136 SOUTH DIPPER LANE, DECATUR, IL 62522

1 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 1/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 1/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
Footnote 23.
★★★★★ 1/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 4/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures — Data not available.

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2024-09-25 19 19 0 152 2 228
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2023-08-22 46 19 33 364 1 364
Total weighted health score
262.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (94)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

3 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
86 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
5 G/H/I Actual harm
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0623 Provide timely notification to the resident, and if applicable to the resident representative and ombudsman, before transfer or discharge, including appeal rights. E 2024-11-13 2024-11-14
0660 Plan the resident's discharge to meet the resident's goals and needs. E 2024-11-13 2024-11-14
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. D 2024-09-25 2024-10-18
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2024-09-25 2024-10-18
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. E 2024-09-25 2024-10-18
0644 Coordinate assessments with the pre-admission screening and resident review program; and referring for services as needed. D 2024-09-25 2024-10-18
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2024-09-25 2024-10-18
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. G 2024-09-25 2024-10-18
0688 Provide appropriate care for a resident to maintain and/or improve range of motion (ROM), limited ROM and/or mobility, unless a decline is for a medical reason. D 2024-09-25 2024-10-18
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. E 2024-09-25 2024-10-18
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2024-09-25 2024-10-18
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2024-09-25 2024-10-18
0759 Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. E 2024-09-25 2024-10-18
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. F 2024-09-25 2024-11-13
0801 Employ sufficient staff with the appropriate competencies and skills sets to carry out the functions of the food and nutrition service, including a qualified dietician. F 2024-09-25 2024-11-13
0804 Ensure food and drink is palatable, attractive, and at a safe and appetizing temperature. D 2024-09-25 2024-10-18
0805 Ensure each resident receives and the facility provides food prepared in a form designed to meet individual needs. D 2024-09-25 2024-10-18
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2024-09-25 2024-11-13
0881 Implement a program that monitors antibiotic use. F 2024-09-25 2024-10-18
0883 Develop and implement policies and procedures for flu and pneumonia vaccinations. D 2024-09-25 2024-10-18
0912 Provide rooms that are at least 80 square feet per resident in multiple rooms and 100 square feet for single resident rooms. F 2024-09-25 2024-10-18
0919 Make sure that a working call system is available in each resident's bathroom and bathing area. D 2024-09-25 2024-10-18
0727 Have a registered nurse on duty 8 hours a day; and select a registered nurse to be the director of nurses on a full time basis. F 2024-09-19 2024-10-08
0557 Honor the resident's right to be treated with respect and dignity and to retain and use personal possessions. D 2024-08-20 2024-09-06
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. D 2024-08-20 2024-09-06
0655 Create and put into place a plan for meeting the resident's most immediate needs within 48 hours of being admitted D 2024-08-20 2024-09-06
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2024-08-20 2024-09-06
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. E 2024-05-08 2024-05-30
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. E 2024-05-08 2024-05-30
0740 Ensure each resident must receive and the facility must provide necessary behavioral health care and services. E 2024-05-08 2024-05-30
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. G 2024-04-04 2024-04-17
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. G 2024-04-04 2024-04-17
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2024-02-10 2024-03-01
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2024-02-10 2024-03-01
0727 Have a registered nurse on duty 8 hours a day; and select a registered nurse to be the director of nurses on a full time basis. F 2024-02-10 2024-03-01
0918 Provide a bathroom in or located near each resident’s room. E 2024-02-10 2024-03-01
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2024-01-26 2024-02-22
0576 Ensure residents have reasonable access to and privacy in their use of communication methods. F 2023-08-22 2023-09-14
0636 Assess the resident completely in a timely manner when first admitted, and then periodically, at least every 12 months. D 2023-08-22 2023-09-14
0640 Encode each resident’s assessment data and transmit these data to the State within 7 days of assessment. E 2023-08-22 2023-09-14
0642 Ensure a qualified health professional conducts resident assessments. E 2023-08-22 2023-09-14
0645 PASARR screening for Mental disorders or Intellectual Disabilities D 2023-08-22 2023-09-14
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2023-08-22 2023-09-14
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2023-08-22 2023-09-14
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. E 2023-08-22 2023-09-14
0801 Employ sufficient staff with the appropriate competencies and skills sets to carry out the functions of the food and nutrition service, including a qualified dietician. C 2023-08-22 2023-09-19
0803 Ensure menus must meet the nutritional needs of residents, be prepared in advance, be followed, be updated, be reviewed by dietician, and meet the needs of the resident. E 2023-08-22 2023-09-14
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2023-08-22 2023-09-14
0912 Provide rooms that are at least 80 square feet per resident in multiple rooms and 100 square feet for single resident rooms. F 2023-08-22 2023-11-01
0924 Put firmly secured handrails on each side of hallways. E 2023-08-22 2023-09-14

Deficiencies 0758 — description Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. 0758.description
Deficiencies 0758 — description Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. 0758.description
